The 10th Amendment to the Constitution: “The powers not delegated to the United States by the Constitution, nor prohibited by it to the States, are reserved to the STATES respectively, or TO THE PEOPLE.” From the Reagan Library: [The 10th Amendment] makes clear that any powers that are not specifically given to the federal government, nor withheld from the states, are reserved to those respective STATES, or TO THE PEOPLE at large...
